response.justification: Bogdan has genuine geospatial experience through GISCollective and a background in distributed, low-latency systems at Dunnhumby, which provides some domain adjacency. However, the role is fundamentally a C++/Rust systems engineering position focused on routing data pipelines and navigation tile production — areas where the candidate has no demonstrated depth. His C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project; his entire subsequent career has been built on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no evidence of routing tile construction, navigation guidance systems, geospatial data transformation at scale, or production mapping pipelines, which are the core deliverables of this role.
strengths: Geospatial domain exposure via GISCollective (open-source, event-driven geo-data platform) and Green Map GIS work
Distributed systems and high-throughput low-latency experience at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O)
Demonstrated breadth across stacks and willingness to work in unfamiliar technical territory
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over a decade stale; no Rust experience whatsoever — both are primary requirements
No exposure to routing tile pipelines, navigation guidance systems, or production map data processing at scale
Geospatial work is hobbyist/NGO-grade data collection, not the performance-critical routing data infrastructure the role demands
